name: Empanaditas
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cottage Cheese; Sm Curd, Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Hard boil an egg and peel it. Chop the boiled egg.
2. In a 10-inch skillet, cook and stir ground beef until brown, breaking it up into small pieces. Drain, reserving 1 tablespoon of fat.
3. Add onion, raisins, olives, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
4. Stir in cottage cheese and the chopped hard-boiled egg.
5. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
6. Mix egg white with water until slightly foamy; set aside.
7. Prepare pastry dough and divide it into two halves. Shape each half into flattened rounds on a lightly floured surface.
8. Roll one round of pastry into a circle about 14 inches in diameter. Cut into 11 or 12 circles, each 3.5 inches in diameter.
9. Spoon about 2 teaspoons of the beef mixture onto the center of each circle. Brush the edge of the pastry with egg white mixture.
10. Fold the pastry circle over the filling and press the edge with a fork to seal.
11. Place empanaditas on an ungreased cookie sheet. Repeat with remaining pastry circles.
12. Gather any remaining pastry, shape into another round, roll, cut, and fill as before.
13. Beat egg yolk with milk until well blended. Brush over tops of empanaditas.
14. Bake in the preheated oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm.
